arts.ttl: Pro-Palestinian protest in Udine ahead of Italy-Israel match
arts.sum: A pro-Palestinian demonstration has begun in Udine, Italy, ahead of tonight’s Nations League football match between Italy and Israel. The protest, which started in Piazza della Repubblica, is organized by several groups including the Pro-Palestine Committee of Udine, the Palestinian Community of Friuli Venezia Giulia and Veneto, and Young Palestinians FVG. Calciomercato report that demonstrators are carrying flags and banners with slogans such as “Let’s kick apartheid” and “Israel out of FIFA,” expressing their opposition to Israel’s participation in international football. The protest march is moving through the city center, beginning at 5 PM local time. arts.ttl: Agent of Acerbi and De Vrij: “They want to stay, but If Inter decides not to keep them…”
arts.sum: Federico Pastorello, the agent representing both Francesco Acerbi and Stefan de Vrij, has addressed the future of the two Inter Milan defenders. With both players’ contracts set to expire on June 30, 2025, speculation has been growing about their potential departures from the Nerazzurri. Speaking before the AIACS (Italian Association of Football Agents and Clubs) electoral assembly, Pastorello provided insight into the situation: “Renewal? It’s still very early, there are clauses that determine the end or continuity of the relationship. It’s our duty to keep our eyes open.
